background: Assume $\hat{\theta}_1$ and $\hat{\theta}_2$ are the two estimators of parameter $\theta$. Suppose that $E\left(\hat{\theta}_1\right)=0.9 \theta, E\left(\hat{\theta}_2\right)=\theta, \operatorname{var}\left(\hat{\theta}_1\right)=2$, and $\operatorname{var}\left(\hat{\theta}_2\right)=3$.

task.answer:
We have
$$
\begin{gathered}
M S E \text { of } \theta_1=\operatorname{var}(\hat{\theta})+(\text { bias })^2=2+0.01 \theta^2 . \\
M S E \text { of } \theta_2=\operatorname{var}(\hat{\theta})+(\text { bias })^2=3+0=3 .
\end{gathered}
$$

Thus, if $|\theta|<10$ then $\hat{\theta}_1$ would be preferred. If $|\theta|>10$ then $\hat{\theta}_2$ is preferred.

task.query: We will later discuss how to establish a lower bound on the variance using an inequality known as the Cramér-Rao inequality.
task.answer: We now discuss some basic methods of parameter estimation.
